seoTitle: Maryville vs Matcham — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Maryville and Matcham. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Maryville and Matcham.
heading: Maryville vs Matcham — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Maryville, New South Wales and Matcham, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Matcham has a lower overall climate risk score (43/100) compared to Maryville (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Maryville or Matcham safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Maryville has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Matcham scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Maryville scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Matcham scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Maryville or Matcham?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Matcham has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
